title: Wet-felted trivet set
subtitle: Three thick merino trivets, 15 cm diameter, for placing under warm dishes on a table
excerpt: Wet-felt three thick discs of merino roving to produce trivets that protect a table surface from warm dishes. Each disc is felted to 1.5 cm thick and 15 cm across. Uses around 60 g of roving per disc. Not heat-resistant for hot pots or baking trays.
sourceNotes: Thick wet-felted discs used as trivets for warm dishes are a standard introductory project in European felting workshops. Observation-led; the construction method is consistent across guild teaching. Note: wool felt is not heat-resistant for pots above 70 degrees C; these trivets are for warm serving dishes, not baking or hob use.

glossaryTerms.definition: Combed and drawn wool fibres in a continuous rope, ready for felting. In wet felting, wisps pulled from the roving are laid in crossing layers before wetting and agitation.
A felting method that uses hot water, soap, and physical agitation to cause wool fibres to lock together into a dense fabric.

content.text: trivets protect table surfaces from warm serving dishes, soup tureens, and bread baskets. Each trivet is felted from

laid six layers thick to produce a disc 1.5 cm thick that absorbs and distributes warmth without marking the table. Wool felt is not heat-resistant: these trivets are for dishes up to approximately 70 degrees C, not for hot pots from the hob, baking trays from the oven, or cast-iron cookware. Use each trivet only for warm serving dishes and table display.

attrs.title: Heat limitation
attrs.body: Wool felt scorches above 70°C. Use for warm dishes only, not hot pans, baking trays, or cast-iron cookware from the hob or oven.

items.name: Merino roving, 21 to 24 micron, in your chosen colour, 60 g per trivet (180 g for a set of 3)
Hot water and olive-oil soap or wool wash
Bamboo rolling mat, at least 40 x 40 cm

content.text: Step 1: Lay the disc (for one trivet)

Mark a 30 cm circle on the rolling mat. Pull wisps of roving 2 to 3 cm wide and lay them across the circle in one direction, overlapping the edges. Add a second layer at 90 degrees. Continue building layers, alternating directions, until six layers are complete. The stack should feel 3 to 4 cm thick before wetting. A thick stack is needed: this trivet will shrink to about 40 percent in each dimension and compress to 1.5 cm thick.

Pour hot soapy water over the disc, covering it completely. Place the tea towel over the top and press down firmly with your palms to work the water through all six layers. Continue pressing for 3 to 4 minutes until the disc feels uniformly wet and the layers have compressed somewhat. Fold the overhanging edges onto the top surface.

Rub the wet surface through the tea towel in circular motions for 10 minutes. Lift the cloth and rub directly on the wet felt for another 10 minutes. The surface should begin to felt and resist pulling apart. Turn the disc over and repeat on the other side. Thick discs need more rubbing time than thin flat panels: the fibres in the interior layers must migrate outward and interlock.

Roll the disc in the mat and roll back and forth on a firm surface 50 times. Unroll, turn 90 degrees, roll 50 more. Alternate directions until the disc measures 18 to 20 cm in diameter. Shock with cold water, then continue rolling for another 50 counts in each direction until the disc reaches 15 cm in diameter and feels very dense. The surface should resist denting when pressed with a thumb.

Rinse thoroughly in cold water, squeeze out excess, and place flat to dry. Drying flat on a clean surface produces a level trivet; drying on a curved surface can introduce a bow. Repeat the process for the remaining trivets in the set.

items.problem: The trivet is too thin and feels floppy
items.cause: Insufficient roving layers or insufficient rolling time
items.fix: A thick, firm trivet needs at least six heavy layers of roving and thorough fulling. Rewet and continue rolling if the disc is still under 15 cm diameter but feels thin.

items.problem: The disc has a smooth side and a lumpy side
items.cause: Only one face was rubbed during the initial felting stage
items.fix: Turn the disc over regularly during rubbing and rolling to felt both surfaces equally.
